narrative: Jindal Stainless was the strongest performer on absolute waste generation, with only 1.63 million MT total waste generated versus SAIL at 12.72 million MT, JSW at 14.41 million MT and Tata Steel at 22.09 million MT. However, Tata’s FY2023 value is consolidated and therefore structurally not comparable with the standalone peer figures. On recovery, Tata led with 20.15 million MT recovered, ahead of JSW and SAIL, showing strong circularity at scale, while SAIL led on low disposal with only 3,671 MT disposed. Jindal was the laggard on total disposal because it reported 766,299.7 MT disposed, largely under other disposal. The structural story is clear: Jindal’s smaller stainless/EAF footprint reduces absolute waste, SAIL’s integrated route generates large slag/dust streams but uses strong recovery practices, and Tata’s consolidated boundary inflates absolute values.
